programme.title: Diplomat's Archives: Hidden Stories
programme.desc: As of April 2025, S. Korea has established diplomatic relations with a total of 194 countries. Among them are countries that have had ties and exchanges with Korea for hundreds of years. With such long-standing relationships with so many nations, might there be fascinating hidden stories between Korea and the rest of the world that we don’t know yet? "Diplomat’s Archives: Hidden Stories" begins with this very question. Together with foreign embassies in Korea, the series uncovers and sheds light on meaningful yet lesser-known stories between Korea and other countries. Through this, we aim to reaffirm the deep friendships that have been built over time, highlight how countries are interconnected—bilaterally and multilaterally— and emphasize the importance of cooperation on the global stage today.

programme.title: VIBE IN ACTION: K-INITIATIVE
programme.desc: The future has already begun. With AI now part of everyday life, technology is no longer a distant concept. Arirang TV’s [Vibe in Action] follows the key future industries driving Korea, capturing where innovation stands today—and where it’s headed. AI · Bio · Climate · Defense · Economy. Through these five keywords, <Vibe in Action> captures the “future moments” we’re living through right now.

programme.title: WITH SCIENCE, WE LIVE
programme.desc: Humanity has learned from the past, transcended the present, and dreamed of the future."With Science, We Live"explores the frontiers of science alongside some of the world’s leading thinkers, offering insights into what lies ahead and uncovering new possibilities.In an era of rapid change, where are we headed?As we navigate between the vast universe and the smallness of human existence, we reconsider the direction we must take.Now, in this very moment, we imagine the future shaped by our choices—and move forward toward it.

programme.title: Beauty On and Off
programme.desc: K-Beauty is loved all around the world, but choosing the “right” product isn’t always easy. With so many reviews out there, it can sometimes feel even more confusing. So two male idols hit the streets themselves! Still new to the world of beauty, they meet everyday Koreans— people who are true “beauty experts” in their daily lives, each with their own routines and must-have products. Inspired by the recommendations they hear on the street, the two idols try the products themselves, sharing honest reviews and comparing their different tastes as they discover what works best for them. Step by step, their experiences and notes come together, eventually forming their very own special “beauty book”. Join two idols on their honest and fun journey as they explore and discover the world of K-Beauty together!

programme.title: The Art of Pairing
programme.desc: Taste the Layer, Feel the Pair.Annyeonghaseyo, Hello, Buongiorno!Every bite in balance. Every table, complete! Chef Fabri is deeply devoted to Korean cuisine,and what he creates is something more than just a meal. Kimchi, bulgogi, gimbap…Korean food is now celebrated around the world as K-food. But real Korean cuisine goes beyond what’s on the plate.It’s about where the ingredients come from,and how they come together. Fabri, an Italian chef who has truly fallen for Korean food,sets out on a journey across Koreain search of the perfect balance of a complete Korean table. Along the way, he meets local people,finds inspiration in each moment,and brings his own culinary instinct to every dish,all coming together as one complete table. This is not just a cooking show.It’s a moment where food, drinks, and people connect,capturing a truly Korean way of dining. Join Chef Fabriand discover the most perfect pairing of K-food.

programme.title: SmartBiz Accelerators
programme.desc: Along with the strengthened manufacturing capabilities of various countries all around the world, "Smart Factory" has garnered a lot of attention in the global market. And the government has unveiled and promoted a roadmap "Manufacturing Innovation 3.0," as part of an effort to enhance the competitiveness of the domestic manufacturing sector. Moreover, a domestic major company Samsung Electronics has provided a smart factory solution in order to boost mutual growth with small and medium-sized businesses. SmartBiz Accelerators introduces a variety of domestic small and medium-sized companies that have achieved continuous growth based on the smart factory project provided by Samsung Electronics as well as gives information on the technological competitiveness and the outlook for the companies in the global market. #SmartBizAccelerators #ArirangTV #smartfactory #smartfactoryproject #competitiveness_of_manufacturing #strengthening_competitiveness_of_SMEs #expanding_into_overseas_markets #growth_into_globalbusiness

programme.title: Regional Tastes
programme.desc: An archive video produced to record the local foods of various regions across South Korea. It shares stories about the recipe, history, and characteristics of each regional dish.   ※ VOD service is currently not available due to copyright issues.

programme.title: HOME TO TABLE
programme.desc: In the rush of everyday life, we often lose sight of the precious moments shared with family. But memories we thought forgotten come back to life, right at the dinner table. In this heartwarming journey, young people learn time-honored Korean home recipes passed down with love from their parents. Discover the rich flavors of family, seasoned by time spent together.

programme.title: K-FOOD CLASS
programme.desc: Chef Joseph Lidgerwood’s class is… A Little Too Special! A K-FOOD experience crafted like no other – Now Open! As global interest in Korean culture continues to thrive, K-FOOD has become one of its most compelling pillars. And today, people aren’t just curious about how it tastes. They want to learn how it’s made. Joseph Lidgerwood, chef-owner of the Michelin two-star restaurant EVETT, is an Australian chef who has developed a deep connection to Korea’s traditional jang and its ingredients. Now, he’s ready to share what he calls the “the true taste of Korea.” Each episode welcomes a new group of participants from around the world, learning the all-time favorites such as Korean fried chicken, jaeyuk bokkeum, ganjang gejang, godeungeo kimchijjim, and bajirak sujebi, all reimagined through Joseph’s unique style. The atmosphere in the kitchen, shaped by curiosity, focus, and genuine interaction between Joseph and his students, often becomes as memorable as the food they create. You’ll take home the recipes, of course. But what truly sets this class apart is the moments shared along the way, the laughter, the discoveries, and the way flavor bridges cultures. Experience the depth, joy, and creativity of K-FOOD in Joseph’s A Little Too Special: K-FOOD Class!

programme.title: The Art of Pairing
programme.desc: Taste the Layer, Feel the Pair.Annyeonghaseyo, Hello, Buongiorno!Every bite in balance. Every table, complete! Along the way, he meets local people,finds inspiration in each moment,and brings his own culinary instinct to every dish,all coming together as one complete table. This is not just a cooking show.It’s a moment where food, drinks, and people connect,capturing a truly Korean way of dining. Join Chef Fabriand discover the most perfect pairing of K-food.

programme.title: ART AND THE CITY
programme.desc: The city we live in is a giant art museum. A full-scale curating talk show held while strolling through the city! We invite you to experience the comfort and healing that art has to offer.※ VOD service is currently not available due to copyright issues.

programme.title: Exploring Korean Literature
programme.desc: 2021 Exploring Korean Literature There is a growing interest in Korean literature among readers from around the world and so we bring Korean classics to Korean modern literature to viewers in this special series under three themes. What They’ve Read Well-known TV personalities read three translated works of Korean literature that have recently received attention. Kim Soom's novel "One Left", brings the comfort women issue to the forefront of the literary world, and her book is recited and discussed by Darcy Paquet, a translator and essayist for the film "Parasite," and translator and essayist Colin Marshall, Italian architect Simone Carena, and fashion designer Shin Ji-hye. The novel was published in the U.S. in September 2020 and a second book print was released this year. Cho Nam-Joo's novel "Kim Jiyoung, Born 1982" has been translated into 25 languages and is recited by TV personality Daniel Hicks (UK), Carlos Gorito (Brazil), YouTuber Sarah Mack (USA) and singer Punita (India) who share their thoughts about the book. Howon, a member of the boy band, W24, recites passages from Kim Young-ha's novel "Diary of a Murderer" and gives commentary. Living to Tell a Story This documentary features Korean writers whose works are not only popular in Korea but also overseas. In episode 1, poet Kim Yi-deum, the Winner of the National Translation Award and the Lucien Stryk Award for her collection of poems, "Hysteria," as well as Korean novelists Kim Cho-Yup and Kim Yeon-su appear to talk about the creative process and how they carry out research before writing a novel. In Episode 2, novelist Yun Ko-Eun, the winner of the Crime Fiction in Translation Dagger award for her book, "The Disaster Tourist", and novelist Ha Seong-nan and poet Jin Eun-young explain what they were trying to convey to readers through their works. In Episode 3, novelist Hwang Sok-young, whose works are mostly inspired by his own experience of living in modern Korean society, tells the story of his life and literary journey. A Word Depicted in Stories The cultural characteristics in Korean literature that encompasses Korean classics to modern novels are highlighted on this show. Six episodes with different themes ranging from female poets in classical literature, makeup culture, wine and arts, tea ceremony, pets, and a literary discussion forum will begin airing on November 5th. -->

programme.title: K-Chat : Conversing in Korean
programme.desc: "What would you say in Korean in a situation like this?" Discover how to express yourself in various daily life situations in Korean, from greetings and making friends to making promises and ordering your favorite food. A comprehensive Korean program designed to teach Korean language through real-life situations! A global initiative featuring top performers. Benefit from clear and friendly explanations by Peter Vint, a British TV personality who mastered Korean himself. Engage in lively Korean language lessons taught by Korean-American idols Aaron and Alexa! Experience a delightful Korean language course that delves into culture, trends, and even generational nuances. Each lesson is a concise 10-minute package, including scenarios, explanations, and practical exercises. Enjoy short, yet entertaining content that will also provide insight into culture, trends, and generational diversity. With just a glance, you'll be able to speak Korean fluently. Embark on an enjoyable and refreshing journey through our Korean language course.

programme.title: SmartBiz Accelerators
programme.desc: Along with the strengthened manufacturing capabilities of various countries all around the world, "Smart Factory" has garnered a lot of attention in the global market. And the government has unveiled and promoted a roadmap "Manufacturing Innovation 3.0," as part of an effort to enhance the competitiveness of the domestic manufacturing sector. Moreover, a domestic major company Samsung Electronics has provided a smart factory solution in order to boost mutual growth with small and medium-sized businesses. SmartBiz Accelerators introduces a variety of domestic small and medium-sized companies that have achieved continuous growth based on the smart factory project provided by Samsung Electronics as well as gives information on the technological competitiveness and the outlook for the companies in the global market. #SmartBizAccelerators #ArirangTV #smartfactory #smartfactoryproject #competitiveness_of_manufacturing #strengthening_competitiveness_of_SMEs #expanding_into_overseas_markets #growth_into_globalbusiness

programme.title: Diplomat's Archives: Hidden Stories
programme.desc: As of April 2025, S. Korea has established diplomatic relations with a total of 194 countries. Among them are countries that have had ties and exchanges with Korea for hundreds of years. With such long-standing relationships with so many nations, might there be fascinating hidden stories between Korea and the rest of the world that we don’t know yet? "Diplomat’s Archives: Hidden Stories" begins with this very question. Together with foreign embassies in Korea, the series uncovers and sheds light on meaningful yet lesser-known stories between Korea and other countries. Through this, we aim to reaffirm the deep friendships that have been built over time, highlight how countries are interconnected—bilaterally and multilaterally— and emphasize the importance of cooperation on the global stage today.

programme.title: PREMIUM COLLECTION
programme.desc: Arirang TV's premium documentary series, Premium Collection※ VOD service is currently not available due to copyright issues.

programme.title: WITH SCIENCE, WE LIVE
programme.desc: Humanity has learned from the past, transcended the present, and dreamed of the future."With Science, We Live"explores the frontiers of science alongside some of the world’s leading thinkers, offering insights into what lies ahead and uncovering new possibilities.In an era of rapid change, where are we headed?As we navigate between the vast universe and the smallness of human existence, we reconsider the direction we must take.Now, in this very moment, we imagine the future shaped by our choices—and move forward toward it.

programme.title: The Art of Pairing
programme.desc: Taste the Layer, Feel the Pair.Annyeonghaseyo, Hello, Buongiorno!Every bite in balance. Every table, complete! Chef Fabri is deeply devoted to Korean cuisine,and what he creates is something more than just a meal. Kimchi, bulgogi, gimbap…Korean food is now celebrated around the world as K-food. But real Korean cuisine goes beyond what’s on the plate.It’s about where the ingredients come from,and how they come together. Fabri, an Italian chef who has truly fallen for Korean food,sets out on a journey across Koreain search of the perfect balance of a complete Korean table. Along the way, he meets local people,finds inspiration in each moment,and brings his own culinary instinct to every dish,all coming together as one complete table. This is not just a cooking show.It’s a moment where food, drinks, and people connect,capturing a truly Korean way of dining. Join Chef Fabriand discover the most perfect pairing of K-food.

programme.title: Beauty On and Off
programme.desc: K-Beauty is loved all around the world, but choosing the “right” product isn’t always easy. With so many reviews out there, it can sometimes feel even more confusing. So two male idols hit the streets themselves! Still new to the world of beauty, they meet everyday Koreans— people who are true “beauty experts” in their daily lives, each with their own routines and must-have products. Inspired by the recommendations they hear on the street, the two idols try the products themselves, sharing honest reviews and comparing their different tastes as they discover what works best for them. Step by step, their experiences and notes come together, eventually forming their very own special “beauty book”. Join two idols on their honest and fun journey as they explore and discover the world of K-Beauty together!

programme.title: VIBE IN ACTION: K-INITIATIVE
programme.desc: The future has already begun. With AI now part of everyday life, technology is no longer a distant concept. Arirang TV’s [Vibe in Action] follows the key future industries driving Korea, capturing where innovation stands today—and where it’s headed. AI · Bio · Climate · Defense · Economy. Through these five keywords, <Vibe in Action> captures the “future moments” we’re living through right now.

programme.title: ART AND THE CITY
programme.desc: The city we live in is a giant art museum. A full-scale curating talk show held while strolling through the city! We invite you to experience the comfort and healing that art has to offer.※ VOD service is currently not available due to copyright issues.

programme.title: Exploring Korean Literature
programme.desc: 2021 Exploring Korean Literature There is a growing interest in Korean literature among readers from around the world and so we bring Korean classics to Korean modern literature to viewers in this special series under three themes. What They’ve Read Well-known TV personalities read three translated works of Korean literature that have recently received attention. Kim Soom's novel "One Left", brings the comfort women issue to the forefront of the literary world, and her book is recited and discussed by Darcy Paquet, a translator and essayist for the film "Parasite," and translator and essayist Colin Marshall, Italian architect Simone Carena, and fashion designer Shin Ji-hye. The novel was published in the U.S. in September 2020 and a second book print was released this year. Cho Nam-Joo's novel "Kim Jiyoung, Born 1982" has been translated into 25 languages and is recited by TV personality Daniel Hicks (UK), Carlos Gorito (Brazil), YouTuber Sarah Mack (USA) and singer Punita (India) who share their thoughts about the book. Howon, a member of the boy band, W24, recites passages from Kim Young-ha's novel "Diary of a Murderer" and gives commentary. Living to Tell a Story This documentary features Korean writers whose works are not only popular in Korea but also overseas. In episode 1, poet Kim Yi-deum, the Winner of the National Translation Award and the Lucien Stryk Award for her collection of poems, "Hysteria," as well as Korean novelists Kim Cho-Yup and Kim Yeon-su appear to talk about the creative process and how they carry out research before writing a novel. In Episode 2, novelist Yun Ko-Eun, the winner of the Crime Fiction in Translation Dagger award for her book, "The Disaster Tourist", and novelist Ha Seong-nan and poet Jin Eun-young explain what they were trying to convey to readers through their works. In Episode 3, novelist Hwang Sok-young, whose works are mostly inspired by his own experience of living in modern Korean society, tells the story of his life and literary journey. A Word Depicted in Stories The cultural characteristics in Korean literature that encompasses Korean classics to modern novels are highlighted on this show. Six episodes with different themes ranging from female poets in classical literature, makeup culture, wine and arts, tea ceremony, pets, and a literary discussion forum will begin airing on November 5th. -->

Moreover, a domestic major company Samsung Electronics has provided a smart factory solution in order to boost mutual growth with small and medium-sized businesses. SmartBiz Accelerators introduces a variety of domestic small and medium-sized companies that have achieved continuous growth based on the smart factory project provided by Samsung Electronics as well as gives information on the technological competitiveness and the outlook for the companies in the global market. #SmartBizAccelerators #ArirangTV #smartfactory #smartfactoryproject #competitiveness_of_manufacturing #strengthening_competitiveness_of_SMEs #expanding_into_overseas_markets #growth_into_globalbusiness

programme.title: TALK ROOM
programme.desc: What is the true strength of the K-people leading the K-Wave? We invite the most talked-about figures—those who influence not just Korea but the world—into our studio for intimate one-on-one conversations. Through their stories, the secrets behind overcoming failure and achieving success, and even private small talk, we uncover what makes them extraordinary. When you listen to someone’s story, you enter their world. In this time of dialogue with the most Korean yet most global K-people, we step into the K-Universe.

programme.title: RE: SPACE
programme.desc: 'Re:Space' explores the hidden value of spaces by uncovering the stories, history, and people behind them.It highlights how old or forgotten places are transformed and given new meaning through creative reuse. Through these spaces, the program connects local culture, community identity, and sustainable regeneration.※ VOD service is currently not available due to copyright issues.

programme.title: Drawing Diary
programme.desc: Take a relaxed stroll around the neighborhood with “Drawing Diary.” Exploring the history of familiar places and uncovering hidden gems, the program captures the everyday scenery of Korea through the artist’s drawings.※ VOD service is currently not available due to copyright issues.

programme.title: Exploring Korean Literature
programme.desc: 2021 Exploring Korean Literature There is a growing interest in Korean literature among readers from around the world and so we bring Korean classics to Korean modern literature to viewers in this special series under three themes. What They’ve Read Well-known TV personalities read three translated works of Korean literature that have recently received attention. Kim Soom's novel "One Left", brings the comfort women issue to the forefront of the literary world, and her book is recited and discussed by Darcy Paquet, a translator and essayist for the film "Parasite," and translator and essayist Colin Marshall, Italian architect Simone Carena, and fashion designer Shin Ji-hye. The novel was published in the U.S. in September 2020 and a second book print was released this year. Cho Nam-Joo's novel "Kim Jiyoung, Born 1982" has been translated into 25 languages and is recited by TV personality Daniel Hicks (UK), Carlos Gorito (Brazil), YouTuber Sarah Mack (USA) and singer Punita (India) who share their thoughts about the book. Howon, a member of the boy band, W24, recites passages from Kim Young-ha's novel "Diary of a Murderer" and gives commentary. Living to Tell a Story This documentary features Korean writers whose works are not only popular in Korea but also overseas. In episode 1, poet Kim Yi-deum, the Winner of the National Translation Award and the Lucien Stryk Award for her collection of poems, "Hysteria," as well as Korean novelists Kim Cho-Yup and Kim Yeon-su appear to talk about the creative process and how they carry out research before writing a novel. In Episode 2, novelist Yun Ko-Eun, the winner of the Crime Fiction in Translation Dagger award for her book, "The Disaster Tourist", and novelist Ha Seong-nan and poet Jin Eun-young explain what they were trying to convey to readers through their works. In Episode 3, novelist Hwang Sok-young, whose works are mostly inspired by his own experience of living in modern Korean society, tells the story of his life and literary journey. A Word Depicted in Stories The cultural characteristics in Korean literature that encompasses Korean classics to modern novels are highlighted on this show. Six episodes with different themes ranging from female poets in classical literature, makeup culture, wine and arts, tea ceremony, pets, and a literary discussion forum will begin airing on November 5th. -->

programme.title: WITH SCIENCE, WE LIVE
programme.desc: Humanity has learned from the past, transcended the present, and dreamed of the future."With Science, We Live"explores the frontiers of science alongside some of the world’s leading thinkers, offering insights into what lies ahead and uncovering new possibilities.In an era of rapid change, where are we headed?As we navigate between the vast universe and the smallness of human existence, we reconsider the direction we must take.Now, in this very moment, we imagine the future shaped by our choices—and move forward toward it.

programme.title: Diplomat's Archives: Hidden Stories
programme.desc: As of April 2025, S. Korea has established diplomatic relations with a total of 194 countries. Among them are countries that have had ties and exchanges with Korea for hundreds of years. With such long-standing relationships with so many nations, might there be fascinating hidden stories between Korea and the rest of the world that we don’t know yet? "Diplomat’s Archives: Hidden Stories" begins with this very question. Together with foreign embassies in Korea, the series uncovers and sheds light on meaningful yet lesser-known stories between Korea and other countries. Through this, we aim to reaffirm the deep friendships that have been built over time, highlight how countries are interconnected—bilaterally and multilaterally— and emphasize the importance of cooperation on the global stage today.

programme.title: K-Chat : Conversing in Korean
programme.desc: "What would you say in Korean in a situation like this?" Discover how to express yourself in various daily life situations in Korean, from greetings and making friends to making promises and ordering your favorite food. A comprehensive Korean program designed to teach Korean language through real-life situations! A global initiative featuring top performers. Benefit from clear and friendly explanations by Peter Vint, a British TV personality who mastered Korean himself. Engage in lively Korean language lessons taught by Korean-American idols Aaron and Alexa! Experience a delightful Korean language course that delves into culture, trends, and even generational nuances. Each lesson is a concise 10-minute package, including scenarios, explanations, and practical exercises. Enjoy short, yet entertaining content that will also provide insight into culture, trends, and generational diversity. With just a glance, you'll be able to speak Korean fluently. Embark on an enjoyable and refreshing journey through our Korean language course.

programme.title: The Art of Pairing
programme.desc: Taste the Layer, Feel the Pair.Annyeonghaseyo, Hello, Buongiorno!Every bite in balance. Every table, complete! Chef Fabri is deeply devoted to Korean cuisine,and what he creates is something more than just a meal. Kimchi, bulgogi, gimbap…Korean food is now celebrated around the world as K-food. But real Korean cuisine goes beyond what’s on the plate.It’s about where the ingredients come from,and how they come together. Fabri, an Italian chef who has truly fallen for Korean food,sets out on a journey across Koreain search of the perfect balance of a complete Korean table. Along the way, he meets local people,finds inspiration in each moment,and brings his own culinary instinct to every dish,all coming together as one complete table. This is not just a cooking show.It’s a moment where food, drinks, and people connect,capturing a truly Korean way of dining. Join Chef Fabriand discover the most perfect pairing of K-food.

programme.title: HOME TO TABLE
programme.desc: In the rush of everyday life, we often lose sight of the precious moments shared with family. But memories we thought forgotten come back to life, right at the dinner table. In this heartwarming journey, young people learn time-honored Korean home recipes passed down with love from their parents. Discover the rich flavors of family, seasoned by time spent together.

In episode 1, poet Kim Yi-deum, the Winner of the National Translation Award and the Lucien Stryk Award for her collection of poems, "Hysteria," as well as Korean novelists Kim Cho-Yup and Kim Yeon-su appear to talk about the creative process and how they carry out research before writing a novel. In Episode 2, novelist Yun Ko-Eun, the winner of the Crime Fiction in Translation Dagger award for her book, "The Disaster Tourist", and novelist Ha Seong-nan and poet Jin Eun-young explain what they were trying to convey to readers through their works. In Episode 3, novelist Hwang Sok-young, whose works are mostly inspired by his own experience of living in modern Korean society, tells the story of his life and literary journey. A Word Depicted in Stories The cultural characteristics in Korean literature that encompasses Korean classics to modern novels are highlighted on this show. Six episodes with different themes ranging from female poets in classical literature, makeup culture, wine and arts, tea ceremony, pets, and a literary discussion forum will begin airing on November 5th. -->

programme.title: The Art of Pairing
programme.desc: Taste the Layer, Feel the Pair.Annyeonghaseyo, Hello, Buongiorno!Every bite in balance. Every table, complete! Chef Fabri is deeply devoted to Korean cuisine,and what he creates is something more than just a meal. Kimchi, bulgogi, gimbap…Korean food is now celebrated around the world as K-food. But real Korean cuisine goes beyond what’s on the plate.It’s about where the ingredients come from,and how they come together. Fabri, an Italian chef who has truly fallen for Korean food,sets out on a journey across Koreain search of the perfect balance of a complete Korean table. Along the way, he meets local people,finds inspiration in each moment,and brings his own culinary instinct to every dish,all coming together as one complete table. This is not just a cooking show.It’s a moment where food, drinks, and people connect,capturing a truly Korean way of dining. Join Chef Fabriand discover the most perfect pairing of K-food.

programme.title: Exploring Korean Literature
programme.desc: 2021 Exploring Korean Literature There is a growing interest in Korean literature among readers from around the world and so we bring Korean classics to Korean modern literature to viewers in this special series under three themes. What They’ve Read Well-known TV personalities read three translated works of Korean literature that have recently received attention. Kim Soom's novel "One Left", brings the comfort women issue to the forefront of the literary world, and her book is recited and discussed by Darcy Paquet, a translator and essayist for the film "Parasite," and translator and essayist Colin Marshall, Italian architect Simone Carena, and fashion designer Shin Ji-hye. The novel was published in the U.S. in September 2020 and a second book print was released this year. Cho Nam-Joo's novel "Kim Jiyoung, Born 1982" has been translated into 25 languages and is recited by TV personality Daniel Hicks (UK), Carlos Gorito (Brazil), YouTuber Sarah Mack (USA) and singer Punita (India) who share their thoughts about the book. Howon, a member of the boy band, W24, recites passages from Kim Young-ha's novel "Diary of a Murderer" and gives commentary. Living to Tell a Story This documentary features Korean writers whose works are not only popular in Korea but also overseas. In episode 1, poet Kim Yi-deum, the Winner of the National Translation Award and the Lucien Stryk Award for her collection of poems, "Hysteria," as well as Korean novelists Kim Cho-Yup and Kim Yeon-su appear to talk about the creative process and how they carry out research before writing a novel. In Episode 2, novelist Yun Ko-Eun, the winner of the Crime Fiction in Translation Dagger award for her book, "The Disaster Tourist", and novelist Ha Seong-nan and poet Jin Eun-young explain what they were trying to convey to readers through their works. In Episode 3, novelist Hwang Sok-young, whose works are mostly inspired by his own experience of living in modern Korean society, tells the story of his life and literary journey. A Word Depicted in Stories The cultural characteristics in Korean literature that encompasses Korean classics to modern novels are highlighted on this show. Six episodes with different themes ranging from female poets in classical literature, makeup culture, wine and arts, tea ceremony, pets, and a literary discussion forum will begin airing on November 5th. -->

programme.title: Beauty On and Off
programme.desc: K-Beauty is loved all around the world, but choosing the “right” product isn’t always easy. With so many reviews out there, it can sometimes feel even more confusing. So two male idols hit the streets themselves! Still new to the world of beauty, they meet everyday Koreans— people who are true “beauty experts” in their daily lives, each with their own routines and must-have products. Inspired by the recommendations they hear on the street, the two idols try the products themselves, sharing honest reviews and comparing their different tastes as they discover what works best for them. Step by step, their experiences and notes come together, eventually forming their very own special “beauty book”. Join two idols on their honest and fun journey as they explore and discover the world of K-Beauty together!

programme.title: K-Chat : Conversing in Korean
programme.desc: "What would you say in Korean in a situation like this?" Discover how to express yourself in various daily life situations in Korean, from greetings and making friends to making promises and ordering your favorite food. A comprehensive Korean program designed to teach Korean language through real-life situations! A global initiative featuring top performers. Benefit from clear and friendly explanations by Peter Vint, a British TV personality who mastered Korean himself. Engage in lively Korean language lessons taught by Korean-American idols Aaron and Alexa! Experience a delightful Korean language course that delves into culture, trends, and even generational nuances. Each lesson is a concise 10-minute package, including scenarios, explanations, and practical exercises. Enjoy short, yet entertaining content that will also provide insight into culture, trends, and generational diversity. With just a glance, you'll be able to speak Korean fluently. Embark on an enjoyable and refreshing journey through our Korean language course.

programme.title: Seoulscape
programme.desc: While the age when late-night shows starring the high-teen stars in the 80s and 90s were the main talk of the town has passed, there still remain places that make us want to relive the moments of our shared interests in the waves of over-the-top platforms and content production. While their fame from the past faded, there are old towns and districts and the people who love them. “Archive Local Documentary” strives to remember and explore the places that still remain to this day or have already faded. ※ VOD service is currently not available due to copyright issues.

programme.title: VIBE IN ACTION: K-INITIATIVE
programme.desc: The future has already begun. With AI now part of everyday life, technology is no longer a distant concept. Arirang TV’s [Vibe in Action] follows the key future industries driving Korea, capturing where innovation stands today—and where it’s headed. AI · Bio · Climate · Defense · Economy. Through these five keywords, <Vibe in Action> captures the “future moments” we’re living through right now.

programme.title: K-FOOD CLASS
programme.desc: Chef Joseph Lidgerwood’s class is… A Little Too Special! A K-FOOD experience crafted like no other – Now Open! As global interest in Korean culture continues to thrive, K-FOOD has become one of its most compelling pillars. And today, people aren’t just curious about how it tastes. They want to learn how it’s made. Joseph Lidgerwood, chef-owner of the Michelin two-star restaurant EVETT, is an Australian chef who has developed a deep connection to Korea’s traditional jang and its ingredients. Now, he’s ready to share what he calls the “the true taste of Korea.” Each episode welcomes a new group of participants from around the world, learning the all-time favorites such as Korean fried chicken, jaeyuk bokkeum, ganjang gejang, godeungeo kimchijjim, and bajirak sujebi, all reimagined through Joseph’s unique style. The atmosphere in the kitchen, shaped by curiosity, focus, and genuine interaction between Joseph and his students, often becomes as memorable as the food they create. You’ll take home the recipes, of course. But what truly sets this class apart is the moments shared along the way, the laughter, the discoveries, and the way flavor bridges cultures. Experience the depth, joy, and creativity of K-FOOD in Joseph’s A Little Too Special: K-FOOD Class!

Still new to the world of beauty, they meet everyday Koreans— people who are true “beauty experts” in their daily lives, each with their own routines and must-have products. Inspired by the recommendations they hear on the street, the two idols try the products themselves, sharing honest reviews and comparing their different tastes as they discover what works best for them. Step by step, their experiences and notes come together, eventually forming their very own special “beauty book”. Join two idols on their honest and fun journey as they explore and discover the world of K-Beauty together!

programme.title: The Art of Pairing
programme.desc: Taste the Layer, Feel the Pair.Annyeonghaseyo, Hello, Buongiorno!Every bite in balance. Every table, complete! Chef Fabri is deeply devoted to Korean cuisine,and what he creates is something more than just a meal. Kimchi, bulgogi, gimbap…Korean food is now celebrated around the world as K-food. But real Korean cuisine goes beyond what’s on the plate.It’s about where the ingredients come from,and how they come together. Fabri, an Italian chef who has truly fallen for Korean food,sets out on a journey across Koreain search of the perfect balance of a complete Korean table. Along the way, he meets local people,finds inspiration in each moment,and brings his own culinary instinct to every dish,all coming together as one complete table. This is not just a cooking show.It’s a moment where food, drinks, and people connect,capturing a truly Korean way of dining. Join Chef Fabriand discover the most perfect pairing of K-food.

programme.title: Seoulscape
programme.desc: While the age when late-night shows starring the high-teen stars in the 80s and 90s were the main talk of the town has passed, there still remain places that make us want to relive the moments of our shared interests in the waves of over-the-top platforms and content production. While their fame from the past faded, there are old towns and districts and the people who love them. “Archive Local Documentary” strives to remember and explore the places that still remain to this day or have already faded. ※ VOD service is currently not available due to copyright issues.

programme.title: K-CHAT: Travel in Korea
programme.desc: Simple and Fun Korean Lessons, “K-Chat: Travel in Korea” What are the most useful expressions visitors to Korea can use right away?From arriving at the airport to finding your hotel and exploring popular travel destinations, this program offers practical expressions for your Korean language journey. It also covers traditional Korean culture and essential travel information, making it an invaluable resource for beginner to intermediate learners! Discover Korean travel expressions in an engaging and entertaining way with skits performed by ONF’s Hyo-jin, Seung-jun, Wyatt, and Alexa. With clear and accessible explanations from Hye-rim of the Wonder Girls, a graduate of Hankuk University of Foreign Studies, you’ll systematically improve your Korean!

programme.title: K-CHAT: Travel in Korea

programme.title: K-Chat : Conversing in Korean
programme.desc: "What would you say in Korean in a situation like this?" Discover how to express yourself in various daily life situations in Korean, from greetings and making friends to making promises and ordering your favorite food. A comprehensive Korean program designed to teach Korean language through real-life situations! A global initiative featuring top performers. Benefit from clear and friendly explanations by Peter Vint, a British TV personality who mastered Korean himself. Engage in lively Korean language lessons taught by Korean-American idols Aaron and Alexa! Experience a delightful Korean language course that delves into culture, trends, and even generational nuances. Each lesson is a concise 10-minute package, including scenarios, explanations, and practical exercises. Enjoy short, yet entertaining content that will also provide insight into culture, trends, and generational diversity. With just a glance, you'll be able to speak Korean fluently. Embark on an enjoyable and refreshing journey through our Korean language course.

programme.title: Fishing in the Museum
programme.desc: Focusing on the collection of the National Folk Museum of Korea, this program seeks to uncover the stories hidden within traditional fishing tools; it also aims tobreak down the boundaries of the museum and bringing these traditional fishing gears to life by visiting the actual sites where they were once used.※ VOD service is currently not available due to copyright issues.

programme.title: Diplomat's Archives: Hidden Stories
programme.desc: As of April 2025, S. Korea has established diplomatic relations with a total of 194 countries. Among them are countries that have had ties and exchanges with Korea for hundreds of years. With such long-standing relationships with so many nations, might there be fascinating hidden stories between Korea and the rest of the world that we don’t know yet? "Diplomat’s Archives: Hidden Stories" begins with this very question. Together with foreign embassies in Korea, the series uncovers and sheds light on meaningful yet lesser-known stories between Korea and other countries. Through this, we aim to reaffirm the deep friendships that have been built over time, highlight how countries are interconnected—bilaterally and multilaterally— and emphasize the importance of cooperation on the global stage today.

programme.title: SmartBiz Accelerators
programme.desc: Along with the strengthened manufacturing capabilities of various countries all around the world, "Smart Factory" has garnered a lot of attention in the global market. And the government has unveiled and promoted a roadmap "Manufacturing Innovation 3.0," as part of an effort to enhance the competitiveness of the domestic manufacturing sector. Moreover, a domestic major company Samsung Electronics has provided a smart factory solution in order to boost mutual growth with small and medium-sized businesses. SmartBiz Accelerators introduces a variety of domestic small and medium-sized companies that have achieved continuous growth based on the smart factory project provided by Samsung Electronics as well as gives information on the technological competitiveness and the outlook for the companies in the global market. #SmartBizAccelerators #ArirangTV #smartfactory #smartfactoryproject #competitiveness_of_manufacturing #strengthening_competitiveness_of_SMEs #expanding_into_overseas_markets #growth_into_globalbusiness

programme.title: Peace Insight
programme.desc: About Peace InsightA program that restores the homogeneity of North and South Korea by finding a common culture between the two countries and enhances the international viewers’ understanding and interest about reunification including issues on the relationship between North and South Korea and policies on reunification.
